input.query: Find any Teams chats discussing punch list completion on Westfield.

output.query: Find any Teams chats discussing punch list completion on Westfield.

results.content:
The document is a transcript of a Teams Direct Message conversation dated April 27, 2026, primarily between Andrew Cannon and Malachi Amico. It serves as a communication log regarding work-related matters, specifically focusing on scheduling and logistical arrangements for ongoing projects.

Key topics discussed include the installation of a shutter by Prodoor, which is scheduled for 10 AM on the same day. Malachi Amico inquires whether someone needs to meet the installer at the site. Additionally, Malachi requests permission to leave work at 3:30 PM to check on doors at Westfield Collective, indicating he expects to return to the office around 5 PM. Andrew Cannon provides sensitive login information for a Fireflies account, which is crucial for accessing certain work-related resources.

Important details include the specific time for the shutter installation and Malachi's proposed schedule for the afternoon. The conversation highlights the need for coordination among team members regarding site visits and installations. Action items include confirming who will meet the Prodoor installer and approving Malachi's request to leave early for the site visit. Overall, the document captures essential logistical planning and communication necessary for project management.

The document is a transcript of a Teams Direct Message conversation dated April 27, 2026, primarily between Andrew Cannon and Malachi Amico. It serves as a communication log regarding work-related matters, specifically focusing on scheduling and logistical arrangements for ongoing projects.

Key topics discussed include the installation of a shutter by Prodoor, which is scheduled for 10 AM on the same day. Malachi Amico inquires whether someone needs to meet the installer at the site. Additional

The document is an email correspondence between Chad Tragesser, a superintendent at Alleato Group, and Austin Pendygraft, the president of Top Notch Pressure Washing Inc. The purpose of the email is to address an outstanding invoice related to cleaning services provided at the Westfield Collective. Austin is following up on the payment, indicating that he has not yet received it and includes a photo of the east wall, which was the last area he was instructed to touch up.

In the email, Chad responds to Austin by looping in Andrew, the Assistant Project Manager (APM), who is expected to assist in resolving the invoice issue. The communication highlights the importance of collaboration among team members to address financial matters promptly. Chad provides his contact information and the addresses of the company's offices in Indianapolis and St. Petersburg, reinforcing the professional nature of the correspondence.

Key details include the date of the email, April 3, 2026, and Austin's request for payment for services rendered, which remains unresolved. The action item is for Andrew to engage with Austin to facilitate the payment process, ensuring that the invoice is addressed efficiently. The email underscores the need for timely communication and resolution of financial obligations in business operations.

The document is an email correspondence between Chad Tragesser, a superintendent at Alleato Group, and Austin Pendygraft, the president of Top Notch Pressure Washing Inc. The purpose of the email is to address an outstanding invoice related to cleaning services provided at the Westfield Collective. Austin is following up on the payment, indicating that he has not yet received it and includes a photo of the east wall, which was the last area he was instructed to touch up.

In the email, Chad resp

Key topics in the email include the acceptance of the accounting review, which likely pertains to financial assessments or audits related to the Westfield Collective. The email does not speci

metadata.title: Email: Accepted: Accounting Review Westfield Collective

results.content:
The document is an email notification confirming the acceptance of a submission related to the "Accounting Review Westfield Collective." It serves as a formal communication from Jesse Dawson at Alleato Group to Andrew Cannon, indicating that the review has been approved for further consideration or action.

Key topics in the email include the acceptance of the accounting review, which likely pertains to financial assessments or audits related to the Westfield Collective. The email does not specify detailed sections but implies that the review will cover essential accounting practices or findings relevant to the collective's financial status. 

Important details include the date of the email, April 24, 2026, which marks the official acceptance. While specific numbers or requirements are not mentioned in the email, the acceptance suggests that prior evaluations or documents were submitted for review, which may contain critical financial data or metrics.

As for action items, the email does not explicitly outline next steps; however, it implies that Andrew Cannon may need to prepare for subsequent discussions or implementations based on the accepted review. It may also necessitate further collaboration between the two parties to address any findings or recommendations stemming from the review.

memories.content: The user prefers detailed, specific updates on project status, risks, and next steps for Vermillion Rise Warehouse.
User prefers high-leverage, action-oriented tasks prioritized daily for Vermillion Rise Warehouse project.
User prefers to have tasks and RFIs associated with specific project names, due dates, and assignees documented clearly.
Verify latest post-April-22 communications and check structured budget, commitments, direct costs, and change orders before quoting exposure dollars for Westfield Collective.
Westfield Collective is engaged in active closeout and commercial coordination with ongoing discussions about LOI/warranty language, change-order signatures, and remaining work.
